content.claim: Since 2013, municipalities lost formal authority over school maintenance to a central agency (KLIK), shifting closure/keep-open decisions from locally accountable mayors to a national bureaucracy nominally insulated from village-level electoral pressure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Does not measure actual political insulation in practice
Formal authority shift does not preclude informal local influence channels
No data on frequency or nature of KLIK closure decisions post-2013
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
